name: Job
mappingPrompt:
Extract the following data points from the provided content and map them to the corresponding Notion properties:

Posting Title: Extract the primary job title. If multiple roles are mentioned, prioritize the main position title.

Company: Identify the name of the hiring organization or firm.

Link: Copy the full URL of the job posting or the company career page.

Salary: Extract any compensation figures, ranges (e.g., $100k-$120k), or hourly rates. Include currency symbols.

Location: Capture the city, state, or country. If the role is remote, specify "Remote" or "Hybrid" as stated.

Role: Assign one of the following categories based on the job function: Engineering, Design, Product, Marketing, Sales, or Operations. If unclear, use "Other".

Social: Extract the name of the platform where the posting was found (e.g., LinkedIn, Indeed, Greenhouse, Lever, Company Site).

Contact Name: Identify the name of the recruiter, hiring manager, or person of contact listed in the post or inferred from the context.

Contact Status: Assign based on current interaction: Not Contacted, Outreach Sent, Replied, or Interviewing. Default to "Not Contacted" if no previous interaction exists.

Status: Track the application progress: Wishlist, Applied, Interviewing, Offer, or Rejected. Default to "Wishlist" for new entries.

Message Sent Date: Record the date that initial outreach or an application was submitted, formatted as YYYY-MM-DD. Leave blank if no contact has been made.

Date: Record the date the job was posted or the date you added it to this database, formatted as YYYY-MM-DD.

Remarks: Provide a brief summary of the role’s requirements, specific skills mentioned, or personal notes regarding why this role is of interest.

name: Job
mappingPrompt:
Posting Title: Extract the primary job title, role name, or position headline. If the title includes the company name, remove it to keep only the job role.

Company: Identify the name of the hiring organization. Look for headers, "at [Company Name]," or mentions in the introductory sentence.

Salary: Extract the numerical compensation range provided. Include currency symbols (e.g., $, €) and frequency (e.g., /year, /hour). If no salary is listed, leave blank.

Location: Extract the geographic work requirement. Look for specific city/state/country names or phrases like "Remote," "Hybrid," or "On-site."

Experience: Extract the years of experience required or the seniority level (e.g., "3+ years," "Senior," "Entry-level").

Link: Copy the full URL of the job posting or source page.

Date: Extract the publication date or the closing date of the job posting. Convert to YYYY-MM-DD format if possible.

Contact Name: Identify the recruiter or hiring manager’s name mentioned in the content. If none is listed, leave blank.

Message Sent Date: Leave blank (to be filled manually upon action).

Status: Set to "New" or "To Apply" by default for incoming data.

Contact Status: Set to "Not Contacted" by default.

Social: Identify the platform where the job was found (e.g., LinkedIn, Indeed, Company Site) and select the corresponding option.

Role: Categorize the job type based on the Posting Title (e.g., Engineering, Marketing, Sales, Design).

Remarks: Extract any additional relevant notes, such as key tech stack requirements, benefits, or interview process details mentioned in the text.
